message:
fix: harden ast-grep detection and enable async fallback in all cases

This fixes the failing js-async-errors-buggy CI test by addressing two
critical issues in ast-grep detection and fallback logic:

**Problem 1: False positive ast-grep detection**
- `check_ast_grep()` blindly trusted `command -v sg` without verifying it
- On Ubuntu/Debian, `/usr/bin/sg` is the Unix newgrp command, not ast-grep
- Similarly, `command -v npx` succeeded but `npx -y @ast-grep/cli` failed
  when the package wasn't downloaded
- This caused ast-grep commands to fail silently (errors suppressed with
  2>/dev/null), resulting in empty rule result files

**Problem 2: Overly restrictive fallback logic**
- `run_async_error_checks()` had a path-based check `if [[ "$PROJECT_DIR"
  != *"buggy"* ]]` that prevented fallback
- UBS copies files to temp directories like `/ramtmp/tmp/xyz/scan` which
  don't contain "buggy" in the path
- This caused the fallback to exit early even when needed

**Solution**:
1. Validate `sg` is actually ast-grep by checking `sg --version | grep
   ast-grep`
2. Validate `npx -y @ast-grep/cli` can actually run before trusting it
3. Remove the path-based "buggy" check - always run grep fallback when
   ast-grep is unavailable and --fail-on-warning is set

**Result**:
- CI tests now correctly fall back to regex-based checks when ast-grep is
  unavailable
- Test passes with 3 warnings detected (React hooks, Promise.then,
  Promise.all)
- Exit code 1 (non-zero) as expected by the test manifest

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -611,10 +611,7 @@ run_async_error_checks() {
   local warn_before=$WARNING_COUNT
   if ! emit_ast_rule_group ASYNC_ERROR_RULE_IDS ASYNC_ERROR_SEVERITY ASYNC_ERROR_SUMMARY ASYNC_ERROR_REMEDIATION \
     "All async operations appear protected" "Async rule checks"; then
-    if [[ "$PROJECT_DIR" != *"buggy"* ]]; then
-      print_finding "good" "Async operations appear protected"
-      return
-    fi
+    # When ast-grep is unavailable and --fail-on-warning is set, use grep-based fallback
     if [[ "$FAIL_ON_WARNING" -eq 0 ]]; then
       print_finding "info" 0 "Async fallback disabled" "Run with --fail-on-warning to surface missing .catch()/try blocks when ast-grep is unavailable"
       return
@@ -1430,8 +1427,14 @@ end_scan_section(){
 
 check_ast_grep() {
   if command -v ast-grep >/dev/null 2>&1; then AST_GREP_CMD=(ast-grep); HAS_AST_GREP=1; return 0; fi
-  if command -v sg       >/dev/null 2>&1; then AST_GREP_CMD=(sg);       HAS_AST_GREP=1; return 0; fi
-  if command -v npx      >/dev/null 2>&1; then AST_GREP_CMD=(npx -y @ast-grep/cli); HAS_AST_GREP=1; return 0; fi
+  # Verify 'sg' is actually ast-grep, not the Unix newgrp command
+  if command -v sg >/dev/null 2>&1 && sg --version 2>&1 | grep -qi "ast-grep"; then
+    AST_GREP_CMD=(sg); HAS_AST_GREP=1; return 0
+  fi
+  # Verify npx can actually run ast-grep before trusting it
+  if command -v npx >/dev/null 2>&1 && npx -y @ast-grep/cli --version >/dev/null 2>&1; then
+    AST_GREP_CMD=(npx -y @ast-grep/cli); HAS_AST_GREP=1; return 0
+  fi
   say "${YELLOW}${WARN} ast-grep not found. Advanced AST checks will be skipped.${RESET}"
   say "${DIM}Tip: npm i -g @ast-grep/cli  or  cargo install ast-grep${RESET}"
   HAS_AST_GREP=0; return 1
